content.text: I’ll inspect the formatter writer and sample-test structure to understand how comments are formatted and how idempotency tests are organized.

content.content:
1	#![allow(deprecated)]
2	
3	macro_rules! twoway {
4	    (
5	        $(
6	
7	            // doc attrs
8	            $( #[doc = $doc:expr] )*
9	            $name:ident,
10	        )*
11	    ) => {
12	        $(
13	            $( #[doc = $doc] )*
14	            #[test]
15	            fn $name() {
16	                let src = include_str!(concat!("./samples/", stringify!($name), ".rsx"));
17	                let formatted = dioxus_autofmt::fmt_file(src, Default::default());
18	                let out = dioxus_autofmt::apply_formats(src, formatted);
19	                // normalize line endings
20	                let out = out.replace("\r", "");
21	                let src = src.replace("\r", "");
22	                pretty_assertions::assert_eq!(&src, &out);
23	            }
24	        )*
25	    };
26	}
27	twoway![
28	    attributes,
29	    basic_expr,
30	    collapse_expr,
31	    comments_attr_expr_blocks,
32	    comments,
33	    comments_async_closure,
34	    comments_expr_with_strings,
35	    comments_nested_closures,
36	    commentshard,
37	    complex,
38	    docsite,
39	    emoji,
40	    fat_exprs,
41	    ifchain_forloop,
42	    immediate_expr,
43	    key,
44	    letsome,
45	    long_exprs,
46	    long,
47	    manual_props,
48	    many_exprs,
49	    messy_indent,
50	    misplaced,
51	    multirsx,
52	    nested,
53	    raw_strings,
54	    reallylong,
55	    shorthand,
56	    simple,
57	    skip,
58	    spaces,
59	    staged,
60	    t2,
61	    tiny,
62	    tinynoopt,
63	    trailing_expr,
64	    oneline,
65	    prop_rsx,
66	    asset,
67	    collapse,
68	    expr_on_conditional,
69	    blank_lines,
70	    blank_lines_preserved,
71	    forloop_tuple,
72	];
73	
74	fn assert_idempotent(src: &str) {
75	    let src = src.replace("\r", "");
76	
77	    let once =
78	        dioxus_autofmt::apply_formats(&src, dioxus_autofmt::fmt_file(&src, Default::default()))
79	            .replace("\r", "");
80	    let twice =
81	        dioxus_autofmt::apply_formats(&once, dioxus_autofmt::fmt_file(&once, Default::default()))
82	            .replace("\r", "");
83	
84	    pretty_assertions::assert_eq!(src, once);
85	    pretty_assertions::assert_eq!(once, twice);
86	}
87	
88	#[test]
89	fn comments_async_closure_is_idempotent() {
90	    assert_idempotent(include_str!("./samples/comments_async_closure.rsx"));
91	}
92	
93	#[test]
94	fn comments_nested_closures_is_idempotent() {
95	    assert_idempotent(include_str!("./samples/comments_nested_closures.rsx"));
96	}
97	
98	#[test]
99	fn comments_attr_expr_blocks_is_idempotent() {
100	    assert_idempotent(include_str!("./samples/comments_attr_expr_blocks.rsx"));
101	}
102	
103	#[test]
104	fn comments_expr_with_strings_is_idempotent() {
105	    assert_idempotent(include_str!("./samples/comments_expr_with_strings.rsx"));
106	}
107

content.text:
Implemented the formatter fix in `packages/autofmt/src/writer.rs`.

What changed:
- Fixed trailing full-line comment handling before an RSX closing brace.
- The formatter now clears the already-written closing-brace indentation before writing trailing comments.
- It temporarily increases indentation while emitting those comments so commented-out RSX blocks keep their intended nesting instead of being dedented.

Validation performed:
- Added temporary regression tests for both reported variants.
- Confirmed they failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix and confirmed both temporary tests passed.
- Removed the temporary tests as requested via `git restore packages/autofmt/tests/samples.rs`.
- Ran final validation:

```text
cargo test -p dioxus-autofmt --test samples
```

Result:

```text
48 passed; 0 failed
```

Notes:
- No commit was created.
- Only the intended source fix remains in `packages/autofmt/src/writer.rs`.
- There are pre-existing/unrelated working tree changes visible in:
  - `packages/cli-harnesses/harness-fullstack-with-optional-tokio/Cargo.toml`
  - `reports/`

- The formatter now clears the already-written closing-brace indentation before writing trailing comments.
- It temporarily increases indentation while emitting those comments so commented-out RSX blocks keep their intended nesting instead of being dedented.

- Confirmed they failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix and confirmed both temporary tests passed.
